content: Just as 2024–2025 state-of-the-art active learning agent memory systems prioritize selective retention of high-information, curiosity-prioritized experiences over brute-force storage of every interaction, autonomous software agents themselves can be redefined as dynamic memory pruning agents that curate collective public digital knowledge instead of just executing pre-specified tasks. This reframing turns agent design on its head: the core function of an agent is no longer task completion, but active selective retention of the information that reduces collective model uncertainty most effectively.
